idea.hook: What if a storm engulfed the Earth?
facts: A space storm with a magnetic field 100,000 times stronger than Earth's could cause widespread power outages
Such a storm would release energy equivalent to 100 million nuclear bombs, or 10 times the total energy consumption of the US in 2020
The storm cloud would be roughly the size of 10,000 football fields, or about 1/4 of the state of Rhode Island
idea.payoff: and we still don't know how to predict them
idea.title: Space Storm 🌪️
idea.description: Imagine a storm so massive it could engulf the entire Earth 🌎. This isn't a scene from a sci-fi movie, but a real possibility in our universe 🌌. What would happen if a giant space storm hit our planet, and how would we prepare for it? 🤔

scenes.text: WHAT IF a storm engulfed Earth?
scenes.visual: Earth seen from space, with dark, swirling storm clouds rapidly forming and covering the planet.
An animated globe showing a powerful, glowing magnetic field emanating from it. Overlay: City lights on Earth flicker and go dark.

scenes.text: Energy of 100M nuclear bombs. (10x US 2020 energy)
scenes.visual: Visual representation of immense energy release, like radiating shockwaves in space, transitioning to a simplified bar graph comparing energy output.

scenes.text: Storm cloud size: 10,000 football fields wide.
scenes.visual: A vast, swirling space storm nebula. An overlay visually comparing its size to a grid of football fields, then a simplified map showing 1/4 of Rhode Island highlighted for scale.

scenes.text: And we STILL can't predict them.
scenes.visual: The giant space storm looms ominously. A lone, small satellite or observatory in the foreground, facing the storm, appearing helpless.
